task_summary: Execute Phase 2 backup hardening plan (dev parity, deploy-wrapper, restore test, GPG docs, Backblaze docs) then AI-audit the result

errors_encountered.error: Disk 100% on RU VPS — 5.7 GB media tar overflowed 6 GB free space
Disk again 100% — same root cause, didn't add pre-check yet
Access denied — .my.cnf user=shopware has no CREATE DATABASE privilege
Sourced backup_telegram_alerts.sh which installed conflicting EXIT trap that double-signaled failure
Connection closed — USA VPS went offline mid-session due to provider network outage (unrelated to my actions, confirmed by 29 min gap after last successful contact + 100% packet loss from RU VPS)

deviations_from_plan: Media backup for dev.bireta.ru was redesigned mid-flight from local-tar to streaming-to-USA after hitting disk 100% twice. Required a new SSH key + wrapper script (accept_media_stream.sh) on USA, which I could not deploy because USA VPS went offline just as I finished writing it. Phase 2A therefore PARTIAL. Phase 2B (Backblaze) remained explicitly blocked on owner (account creation). Phase 2E (GPG) only docs written, no keypair generated.
self_quality_rationale: Plan was sound and most scripts deployed+tested successfully (safe_exec + restore_verify work correctly). But I pushed on the dev media tar twice into a 6 GB free-space window without adding a disk pre-check FIRST — that was avoidable. Crisis response when USA went offline was clean (invoked AI-audit, stopped all action, gave owner 5-step plan). Owner got frustrated twice, which indicates communication gaps. Overall: architecture OK, operational discipline below bar.
open_issues: USA VPS (biretos.ae) offline — waiting for provider network recovery, owner has visual confirmation of provider-side 'Network Lost' status
Phase 2A dev.bireta.ru media backup not working end-to-end — accept_media_stream.sh written but not deployed on USA (USA offline)
Phase 2B Backblaze B2 blocked on owner account creation
Phase 2E GPG encryption blocked on owner generating keypair locally
safe_exec.sh deployed but not integrated into any caller yet — it guards destructive commands only when explicitly invoked via `safe_exec 'cmd'` or symlink `safe`
Weekly restore_verify cron installed on biretos.ae but cannot run until USA back online
dev_backup.sh has pre-check for disk >90% abort now, but the two failed runs happened before this guard was added
notable_decisions: Chose to stream dev media tar directly to USA VPS (no local copy) after second disk-full event, giving up 3-2-1 for dev media in favor of operational feasibility
Generated separate SSH keys per function (backup_to_ru, backup_to_usa, media_stream_to_usa) instead of one shared key, to limit blast radius if any single key leaks
Used rrsync wrapper with -wo (write-only) + from=IP restriction on authorized_keys for all inter-VPS keys
Paused Phase 2 completely when USA went offline rather than try to continue on dev alone, per AI-audit recommendation
Invoked general-purpose subagent for incident-response AI-audit instead of doing reasoning myself, to get independent second opinion with less recency bias
